prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde (4 atoms: O, C, 2×H) using DFT with B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis (tier standard, density-fit), PySCF backend, gas phase, neutral singlet ground state and doublet cation/anion states. Mulliken partial charges computed for neutral, +1 (cation, *S* = 1/2), and −1 (anion, *S* = 1/2) states. Fukui indices ƒ⁺, ƒ⁻, ƒ⁰ and dual descriptor derived from charge differences. All SCF runs converged; Fukui charge conservation verified (sum ƒ⁺ = 1.0000, sum ƒ⁻ = 1.0000). No warnings. Integrity status: trustworthy.

**Most electrophilic atom:** Carbon (C, index 1, label 2), ƒ⁺ = 0.708, dual descriptor = +0.596 — primary nucleophilic-attack site.

**Most nucleophilic atom:** Oxygen (O, index 0, label 1), ƒ⁻ = 0.467, dual descriptor ≈ 0 — lone-pair donor site.

**Visualization:** `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260706-174614_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`

**Command:** `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpvm_2e3gg/live.json`</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.7083913
